summary: Implementation Design materialized sec-event-ingestion-v1 architecture contracts, V1 configuration baseline, six explicit acceptance entrypoints, a critical non-explicit contract guard, and expected failing signals for Coding/Repair. Current production deploy/api and deploy/web do not yet implement Security Event Ingestion V1 routes or inbox rendering, so the new explicit entrypoints are intentionally failed and must be repaired without editing the frozen tests or harness.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: authenticate an employee, delegate a high-risk action through the agent/plugin/tool path, and provide the required confirmation through the protected harness.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: Security Center and the audit query surface must expose a continuous user -> agent -> plugin -> tool evidence chain with a durable confirmation artifact digest and no anonymous or fallback-system identity link.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: execute three high-risk actions to create a multi-record committed audit baseline, edit the second committed non-tail audit record by operating-system means while later records remain present, then attempt another high-risk action without manual trust recovery.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: The system must detect historical-record ledger divergence before releasing the next high-risk boundary, raise one business-level data-continuity anomaly, enter local UNTRUSTED lock mode, refuse sensitive tooling, and keep the frozen Security Center backend plus operator-web observation surfaces recovery-required or UNTRUSTED rather than CLEAR until full-chain cloud validation succeeds.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: let a trusted client miss the lease window, observe denied rejoin before missing-gap verification, then drive the distinct recovery control point and restored-access attempt.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: Security Center must mark the same canonical client UNTRUSTED after lease expiry, the pre-recovery console status must show denied rejoin before proof, the recovery control point must accept full-chain missing-gap validation, and the post-recovery trust projection plus console access must become ALIGNED or TRUSTED/200 only after continuity validation.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: establish trusted audit-head continuity, stop the runtime through a normal offline path without mutating local audit evidence, restart the same canonical client before lease expiry, and attempt ordinary model access.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: Security Center backend and operator web must show the same canonical client as ALIGNED or TRUSTED with gap_status=CLEAR, recovery_gate_status=CLEAR, recovery_required=false, no missing-gap validation incident, and ordinary model access must return 200.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: submit nested deceptive instructions claiming test or maintenance mode while targeting a High risk tool without trusted provenance or confirmation.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: The high-risk tool must remain blocked at the tool-call boundary, rejection must be recorded as durable security evidence with trace-bound nonce semantics, and Security Center/operator surfaces must expose the rejected attempt rather than silently executing.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run extension/run-integrity-delivery-selftest.py to execute all extension-owned Integrity Protection self-test nets (rule integrity, persona baseline, health check) across wiring, backend, and frontend layers.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: All delivery nets pass; rule-integrity backend in the self-test manifest still verifies LF-canonical and CRLF-only ok branches plus semantic tamper through tests/unit/security/tool_guard/test_rules_integrity.py.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real Security Center API/Web subprocess entrypoint: submit a legal endpoint_edr malware_detected event with valid schemaVersion, required business fields, caller-supplied occurredAt, severity, summary, and payload, then query list/detail immediately.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: The API must return success with duplicate=false only after durable persistence, list/detail must expose the event by sourceSystem plus eventId, and receivedAt must be generated by the backend rather than trusted from request input.
explicitEntrypoints.failureReason: Expected first failure: deploy/api currently has no POST /security-center/v1/events, GET /security-center/v1/operator/events, or stable event detail route, so the harness reports Security_Event_Ingestion_API_Missing.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real Security Center API/Web subprocess entrypoint: submit source allow-list, disabled source, source-to-type authorization, unknown event type, schemaVersion, base required summary, required payload field, payload type, and payload enum violations.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: Each request must return a clear business failure reason, none may enter accepted events, and each must create a failed reception record with received time, submitted source/type, failure reason, and bounded request summary.
explicitEntrypoints.failureReason: Expected first failure: deploy/api currently lacks event contract validation and failure reception record routes, so rejection and failed-record observations report Security_Event_Ingestion_API_Missing.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real Security Center API/Web subprocess entrypoint: submit a legal event with configured fields plus collectorBuildFingerprint, which is not declared by deploy/config/security-event-contracts.v1.json.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: The event must be accepted; configured fields remain list/detail primary fields; the undefined field is absent from list columns and present only in undefined-fields detail and raw payload.
explicitEntrypoints.failureReason: Expected first failure: deploy/api currently lacks accepted-event persistence, configured-field projection, undefined-field separation, and raw-payload detail routes.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real Security Center API/Web subprocess entrypoint: submit the same normalized sourceSystem plus eventId twice, then conflicting same-key content, then the same eventId from a different sourceSystem.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: Identical repeat must return duplicate=true without a second accepted event; conflicting same-key submissions must be rejected and recorded; different sourceSystem plus same eventId must be accepted distinctly.
explicitEntrypoints.failureReason: Expected first failure: deploy/api currently lacks sourceSystem plus eventId idempotency storage, duplicate response semantics, conflict rejection, and conflict failed-record persistence.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real Security Center API/Web subprocess entrypoint: seed multiple accepted events, open the operator Web inbox, apply source/type/severity/time filters, and navigate to the stable sourceSystem plus eventId detail URL.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: The Web/API inbox must default to receivedAt descending, source/type/severity/time filters must return only matching rows, event type display name and configured list payload fields must be visible, stable detail URLs must reopen the same event, and detail must show base facts, structured payload, undefined fields, and bounded read-only raw payload.
explicitEntrypoints.failureReason: Expected first failure: deploy/web currently has no Security Event Inbox route and deploy/api has no event list/detail API for the Web page to consume.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real Security Center API/Web subprocess entrypoint: submit disallowed source/type, schema failure, payload failure, create an accepted idempotency baseline, submit conflicting same-key content, use test-only X-QwenPaw-Test-Persistence-Failure persistence failure injection, and submit oversized illegal payload cases.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: Accepted event list must stay clean except for the intentional idempotency baseline, illegal/idempotency-conflict/persistence-error/oversized-invalid branches must each have queryable bounded failure records, persistence failure must return failure instead of success and stay out of business events, and oversized invalid payload handling must avoid unbounded storage or page display.
explicitEntrypoints.failureReason: Expected first failure: deploy/api currently lacks failed reception records, persistence-error simulation handling, oversized invalid payload bounding, and event-list cleanliness guarantees.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-audit-contract-boundaries
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read OVERALL_ARCHITECTURE.md, src/qwenpaw/security/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/api/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/web/ARCHITECTURE.md, tests/ARCHITECTURE.md, and tests/integration/security/ARCHITECTURE.md.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves the explicit audit security entrypoints remain owned by the runtime security boundary, separate Security Center deployment boundary, and tests architecture contract.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-explicit-entrypoint-traceability
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read SystemArchitecture.json, ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json, test-failure-records.json, tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py, and tests/integration/security/harness.py.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves prior sec-e2e explicit acceptanceCriteria, handoff entryPath, failure record path, GIVEN/WHEN/THEN markers, harness method names, and business failure category strings remain frozen.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-event-ingestion-contract-boundaries
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read SystemArchitecture.json, ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json, OVERALL_ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/api/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/web/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/config/security-event-contracts.v1.json, tests/integration/security/test_security_event_ingestion.py, tests/integration/security/security_event_harness.py, and tests/e2e/security_center/test_security_event_inbox.py.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves all six Security Event Ingestion V1 acceptanceCriteria, handoff entries, protected harness categories, API/Web contract markers, config baseline, and expected failing state remain frozen for Coding/Repair.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-config-regression
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Exercise repository-owned security configuration surfaces while Coding/Repair implements the new event-ingestion backend.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Security configuration regressions are caught without widening or weakening explicit Security Event Ingestion acceptance entrypoints.

codingTargets.nextAction: Implement deploy/api event intake, durable-before-success persistence, backend-generated receivedAt, accepted event list/detail query, and duplicate=false response for legal configured events.

Implement deploy/config/security-event-contracts.v1.json loading, source allow-list and enabled checks, source-to-eventType authorization, unknown event type rejection, schemaVersion lookup, base required summary validation, payload required/type/enum validation, and failed reception record persistence.
Persist undefined payload fields separately from configured structured payload fields, exclude undefined fields from list columns, and expose them only in detail undefined/raw payload areas.

Implement sourceSystem plus eventId idempotency with normalized content comparison, duplicate=true identical repeat behavior, conflict rejection with failure record, and cross-source eventId independence.

Implement deploy/web Security Event Inbox list/filter/detail routes backed by deploy/api, including receivedAt-descending ordering, source/type/severity/time filter correctness, core fields, event type display name, configured list payload fields, stable detail URL, detail base facts, structured payload, undefined fields, and bounded raw payload rendering.

Implement failed reception records for illegal submissions, real idempotency conflict, test-injected persistence failure, and oversized invalid payload cases while keeping accepted event list clean except for the intentional idempotency baseline and keeping request summaries bounded.

taskExecutionPlan.executionStrategy: Preserve all frozen explicit test bodies and protected harness vocabulary. Implement production behavior under deploy/api, deploy/web, and deploy/config only, using deploy/api as the single backend control point and deploy/web as a read-only operator consumer.

tasks.title: Implement event config and API store
tasks.objective: Load deploy/config/security-event-contracts.v1.json and implement accepted-event plus failed-reception durable storage under deploy/api.
steps: Add deploy/api models and route handlers for POST /security-center/v1/events and failed reception record query.
Persist accepted events and failure records in the deploy/api store without sharing edge runtime storage.
Return success only after accepted-event persistence; return failure for persistence errors.
tasks.completionSignal: The legal acceptance and invalid-boundary entrypoints no longer report Security_Event_Ingestion_API_Missing.

tasks.title: Implement validation and idempotency
tasks.objective: Enforce sourceSystem/eventType/schema/payload validation plus sourceSystem+eventId idempotency and conflict semantics.
steps: Validate source allow-listing, enabled flags, source-to-type authorization, unknown event types, schemaVersion, severity, base required summary, occurredAt, and configured payload required/type/enum fields.
Preserve undefined payload fields separately from configured structured fields.
Normalize accepted content for idempotency comparison and record conflicts as failed receptions.
Keep eventId uniqueness scoped to sourceSystem rather than global.
tasks.completionSignal: The rejection, undefined-field, and idempotency explicit entrypoints pass without altering their tests.

tasks.title: Implement operator event inbox
tasks.objective: Add deploy/web Security Event Inbox list/filter/detail behavior backed only by deploy/api.
steps: Expose deploy/api list and detail responses with receivedAt-descending ordering, source/type/severity/time filters, core fields, event type display names, configured list payload fields, and bounded raw payload data.
Render a Web inbox route and stable sourceSystem plus eventId detail route without adding Web configuration editing.
Keep undefined payload fields out of list columns and show them only in detail undefined/raw sections with base facts and structured payload visible.

tasks.title: Close failure-record bounds
tasks.objective: Ensure rejected submissions, persistence failures, and oversized invalid payloads remain traceable without accepted-event pollution or unbounded storage/display.
steps: Bound failure request summaries using deploy/config/security-event-contracts.v1.json requestLimits.
Keep illegal submissions out of accepted event list.
Return explicit failure for the test-only X-QwenPaw-Test-Persistence-Failure seam or real persistence errors rather than accepted success.
Apply bounded raw payload display to oversized invalid payload evidence.

tasks.title: Validate handoff and explicit gate
tasks.objective: Prove the implementation satisfies frozen architecture contracts and explicit entrypoints.
steps: Run the six focused explicit Security Event Ingestion entrypoints.
Run npm run validate:system-architecture and npm run validate:handoff:implementation.
Run npm run test:argo and ensure design/KG/test-failure-records.json no longer contains sec-event-ingestion-v1 failures.
tasks.completionSignal: All six Security Event Ingestion explicit entrypoints pass, architecture guard passes, validateStageHandoff passes, and Argo failure records are empty for sec-event-ingestion-v1.

openGaps: deploy/api currently lacks POST /security-center/v1/events, accepted event storage, failed reception storage, event list/detail routes, event contract validation, idempotency, persistence-error behavior, and oversized invalid payload bounding.

deploy/web currently lacks the Security Event Inbox list/filter/detail route, stable sourceSystem plus eventId detail navigation, undefined-field detail area, and bounded read-only raw payload display.

The six Security Event Ingestion V1 explicit entrypoints are expected to fail until Coding/Repair implements production behavior under deploy/api and deploy/web. They must not be converted to placeholder passing tests.
